entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Students review the parts of speech and learn the components involved in writing a good sentence.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Language Study: Grade 5

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Students can take this course even if they haven't taken grammar before. The beginning is written with a built-in review.

rows.label: Grammar: Grade 5
rows.books: Practice Island||Practice Island Teacher Manual (select pages)||Sentence Island

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Grammar: Grade 5
☐ Bookmark Practice Island Teacher Manual with answers to practice sentences. (Find in Quick Links)

blocks.text: Link back to the previous lesson.

Read the assigned pages and discuss new ideas. It is important for students to be able to talk about the concepts in their own words. The pictures and diagrams in the book can serve as a springboard for discussions about the grammar concepts.
Complete practice examples in the text and/or the practice book that accompany the lesson.
Observe examples of concepts in books being read and in daily life.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Introduction
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment. Check your work. (Refer to Grammar Island if necessary.) Practice Island Sentence 45  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.7-17

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
What Is a Sentence?
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment. Check your work. Practice Island Sentence 46  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.18-20 

➜ DISCUSS
Do the tree's words form a sentence? How do you know?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
What Is a Sentence?
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment. Check your work. Practice Island Sentence 47  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.22-23

➜ DISCUSS
Do the ostriches' words form a sentence? How do you know?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
Two Parts
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 48  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.24-28 

➜ PRACTICE
Draw a vertical line and write a two-word sentence. Is it an idea? Does it say something about something? What part of speech is each word?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
Confusion
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 49  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.29-30 

➜ DISCUSS
What are you wondering?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
Subject & Predicate
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 50  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.31-33 

➜ DISCUSS
Copy the diagram on p.32. Explain how it shows what a sentence is.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
The Noun System
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 51  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.34-39

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 8
lessons.body:
The Verb System
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 52  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.40-43

➜ DISCUSS
Study the diagram on p.43. What do you understand? What is still unclear to you?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
Adverbs
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 53  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.44-45

➜ PRACTICE
Draw a chart with three columns labeled "Verb," "Adverb," and "Adjective." Write at least two examples of adverbs modifying each.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
8 Parts of Speech
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 54  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.46-47 

➜ PRACTICE
See if you can write a sentence that contains all eight parts of speech.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 11
lessons.body:
Interjections
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 55  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.48-50

➜ RECAP
Write what Mud knows about sentences so far. Keep this for the next lesson.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 12
lessons.body:
Complete Sentences
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 56  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.1 p.52-56 

➜ EVALUATE
Compare what you wrote last time to what the author wrote. Did you leave anything out? Which of Mud's sentences have both a subject and a predicate?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
Oopsy and Daisy
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 57  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.58-63 

➜ DISCUSS
Look carefully at the sentences Oopsy and Daisy give Mud on p.63. What are they trying to explain?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
Parts of a Sentence
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 58  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.64-67

➜ DISCUSS
Copy the diagram on p.66 and explain it to someone.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
Action Verbs
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 59  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.68-71  

➜ PRACTICE
Write a sentence that contains an action verb that flows to a direct object. Write another sentence that contains an action verb that does not flow to a direct object.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
Linking Verbs
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 60  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.72-74 

➜ PRACTICE
Write two sentences with linking verbs. Underline the subject complements.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 17
lessons.body:
Linking Verbs (cont.)
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 61  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.75-77

➜ PRACTICE
Write a sentence with an action verb and a direct object. Write another with a linking verb and subject complement.

➜ NOTE
"Complement" means "complete." "Compliment" means to say something nice.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
Doing and Being
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 62  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.78-79 

➜ PRACTICE
Copy the sentence: "Each sentence is an idea in which the subject (noun or pronoun) DOES something or IS something." Is this a "doing sentence" or a "being sentence"?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
Indirect Objects
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.80-82

➜ DISCUSS
Copy the diagram on p.82 and explain it to someone.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
Indirect Objects (cont.)
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.83-84

➜ PRACTICE
Write two sentences that contain indirect objects.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
Composition
 
➜ PRACTICE
Write a description about something beautiful. In each sentence, keep a perfect balance between the number of words in the subject and the number of words in the predicate. If the subject part has three words, then the predicate part has three words also.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 22
lessons.body:
Composition
 
➜ PRACTICE
Write a short story about two characters. One can only speak in subjects, and the other can only speak predicates. The second character finishes all of the first's sentences.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 23
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Sentence Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 24
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island
 
➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Practice Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Pronouns
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 64

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.85-89 "Mud looked curiously" - "said Oopsy."

➜ DIAGRAM
Copy the diagram with wordlists on p.88. Post it somewhere you can refer to easily, as this is an area where frequent mistakes are made in spoken and written language. Listen this week for subject/object mix-ups in conversation.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
Main Sentence Parts
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 65

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.90-94 "All right," - "they were gone."

➜ PRACTICE
Look at a recent written narration. Find a sentence that could be made better by replacing many words that are just ok with a few great words.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 27
lessons.body:
A Simple System
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 66

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.96-100 "Mud stood alone" - "system very well."

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 28
lessons.body:
Clear Sentences
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 67

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.101-105 "A sentence is" - "the blue waves."

➜ PRACTICE
Study the sentence on p.105. Try adding and taking away words. Do you think either improves the sentence?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 29
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 68

➜ PRACTICE
Circle all the subjects and underline all the objects. Check your work.
Sentence Island p.106 
∞ Link: Answer Key p.106-107

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 30
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 69

➜ PRACTICE
Find the three incorrect sentences and rewrite them correctly. Check your work.
Sentence Island p.107
∞ Link: Answer Key p.106-107

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.2 p.108 "Mud looked" - "understand that."

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 31
lessons.body:
Subject-Verb Agreement
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 70

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.3 p.110-117 "Mud walked down" - "Get it?"

➜ DISCUSS
Tell what you are thinking so far about what the cricket is trying to explain.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 32
lessons.body:
Subject-Verb Agreement
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 71

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Sentence Island Ch.3 p.118-125 "Fidget looked" - "respectfully quiet."

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 33
lessons.body:
Subject-Verb Agreement
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP
Practice Island Sentence 72

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.3 p.126-129 "Get it, Mud?" - "Mud after all."

➜ PRACTICE 
Fix this sentence: The deep rhythmic sound of drumming reach the hobbits in the hall.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 34
lessons.body:
Beauty in Writing
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 73

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.3 p.130-136 "You know," - "beautiful images:"

➜ DISCUSS 
Review each diagram on p.137. What does each reveal about what a sentence is?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 35
lessons.body:
Review
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 74

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.3 p.138-146 "Mud was beginning" - "heard a clatter!"

➜ DISCUSS 
How is Mud doing so far with his sentence-writing?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 36
lessons.body:
Word Placement
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 75

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.4 p.148-159 "Mud heard another" - "close to waves."

➜ DISCUSS 
Explain how word placement affects the meanings of these sentences:

-Dark and cloudy, the bird flew into the sky.
-The bird flew into the dark and cloudy sky.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 37
lessons.body:
Word Placement
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 76

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.4 p.160-171 "What words mean" - "on the sand."

➜ PRACTICE 
Make this sentence better: 
Chewing his gum vigorously, a fly buzzed past Joe.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 38
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 77

➜ PRACTICE 
Is there a misplaced modifier in any of these sentences? If so, fix them.

-Joey saw a fly buzz past, humming like a little saw.
-Joey heard the fly on the table zoom away noisily.
-On the table, Joey heard the fly zoom away noisily.
-Dropping quickly, the dancer got out of the way of the curtain.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 39
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 78

➜ PRACTICE 
Are all these sentences correct? Fix the ones that are not.

-The pelican skimming the tops of the waves rise slowly in the breeze.
-The babies in the nursery is asleep.
-The artists in the street paint beautifully.
-At this time the use of personal cell phones are now permitted.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 40
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 79

➜ PRACTICE 
Fix these sentences:

-The look of the buttons have completely changed.
-The riot of galloping wildebeests make difficult targets for the crocs.
-The statement of facts speak for themselves.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 41
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 80

➜ DISCUSS
"If you see an adjective, kill it." -Mark Twain
"The adverb is not your friend." -Stephen King

Explain how modifiers can both help and hurt sentences. How can you ensure your modifiers are helpful and not harmful to your sentences?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 42
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 81

➜ PRACTICE 
Write five sentences that have five words or fewer. Then modify the subjects by adding prepositional phrases between subjects and verbs.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 43
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 82

➜ PRACTICE 
Write a short dialogue between two characters. One can only speak using action verbs, and the other can only speak using linking verbs.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 44
lessons.body:
Practice

➜ PRACTICE 
Write a story about Mud that is funny because each sentence has a misplaced modifier.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 45
15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 46
lessons.body:
Composition

➜ PRACTICE 
Finish rewriting your narration using sentences that are strong and correct. Make sure every sentence begins with a capital letter and ends with an end mark.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 47
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Sentence Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 48
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Practice Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 49
lessons.body:
Review
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 84

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.4 p.172-177 "Mud looked out" - "the question."

➜ PRACTICE 
Write your own example of each kind of sentence on p.176-177.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 50
lessons.body:
Review
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 85

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.4 p.178-188 "He thought about" - "a strange noise."

➜ DISCUSS
Explain Mud's thought process while crafting his sentence. How did it help him compose a good sentence?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 51
lessons.body:
Completeness
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 86

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.5 p.190-197 "Mud looked up" - "cried Herman."

➜ DISCUSS 
Look at Merv's sentences. What is wrong with them? Do they have subjects and predicates? What is missing in each?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 52
lessons.body:
Completeness
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 87

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.5 p.198-203 "'Merv,' said Mud," - "and then down."

➜ DISCUSS 
Look again at Merv's sentences on p.197. Add words to make them complete thoughts.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 53
lessons.body:
4 Types of Sentences
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 88

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.5 p.204-209 "'So,' said Mud," - "name was Mud."

➜ PRACTICE 
Write an example of each of the four types of sentences using correct end punctuation.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 54
lessons.body:
Review
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 89

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.5 p.210-213 "Mud looked at" - "you have learned."

➜ DISCUSS 
What is wrong with each sentence?

-During the storm last night.
-With soft fur, the boy petted the kitten.
-Enjoyed the game they were playing.
-The paint on the walls dry quickly.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 55
lessons.body:
Review
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 90

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.5 p.214-226 "It had been" - "from the ocean."

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 56
lessons.body:
Sound
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 91

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.6 p.229-239 "Suddenly, Mud heard" - "said Mud, 'sound.'"

➜ DISCUSS 
Compare the two sentences about thunder. Why does Mud's second sentence sound so much better than the first?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 57
lessons.body:
Two Kinds of Sounds
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 92

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.6 p.240-243 "'Sound,' said" - "y, and z!"

➜ PRACTICE 
Write a sentence about rain that plays with sound.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 58
lessons.body:
Rhythm
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 93

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Sentence Island Ch.6 p.244-251 "So you can" - "all those choices."

➜ DISCUSS 
Write a sentence that uses sound and rhythm to mimic the sound of a bunny's hopping.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 59
lessons.body:
Review
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 94

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
As you read, try to figure out for yourself which sentences are better and why before reading Mud's explanation.
Sentence Island Ch.6 p.252-255 "'Yes,' said" - "a beautiful friendship."

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 60
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 95

➜ PRACTICE 
Write five sentences. Then take away part of each sentence to make the thought incomplete.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 61
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 96

➜ PRACTICE 
Write a short story about a boy who always falls asleep in the middle of his sentences.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 62
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 97

➜ PRACTICE
Imagine a waterfall. Create a list of words that sound like a waterfall. The meanings can be ignored for now. Just focus on sound. Keep this list for a future lesson.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 63
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 98

➜ PRACTICE 
Using your list of words that sound like a waterfall, try to compose 1-2 sentences that sound like a waterfall. Was it easy to do? Did it work well so that the sentence(s) created a waterfall atmosphere?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 64
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island

➜ WARM UP 
Practice Island Sentence 99

➜ PRACTICE 
Write a few sentences that use sound and rhythm to vividly describe a fireworks display.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 65
lessons.body:
Composition

➜ PRACTICE 
Choose a narration (at least a page long) from this term to revise. Look for incomplete thoughts, sentence fragments, and subject-verb disagreement as you read. Mark them on your paper and then write those sentences correctly.

➜ NOTE
You will work on this revision for several weeks.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 66
lessons.body:
Composition

➜ PRACTICE 
Look again at your narration from last time. Circle any misplaced modifiers and then write those sentences correctly. Notice each pronoun. Are subject and object pronouns used correctly? Fix any mistakes.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 67
lessons.body:
Composition

➜ PRACTICE 
Write a clean second draft of your narration.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 68
lessons.body:
Composition

➜ PRACTICE 
Look at your second narration draft. Authors write many drafts to get the right effects. Look at your sentences. Could any be improved by getting rid of unnecessary words? Strike through those. Read your draft aloud. Where could you use sound and rhythm to enhance the meaning? How else could you make your piece more beautiful? Make notes.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 69
lessons.body:
Composition

➜ PRACTICE 
Write a clean third draft of your narration. Check for capital letters and end punctuation.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 70
lessons.body:
Composition

➜ PRACTICE 
Read your narration aloud one more time. Make any final changes you would like to make. Then read it aloud expressively to someone else.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 71
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Sentence Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 5 - Lesson 72
lessons.body:
Exam Week
⍞ Materials: Sentence Island, Practice Island

➜ EXAMS

terms.content:
· Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment.
Practice Island: Sentence 63

· Sentence Island: Write two sentences. One should have an action verb, and the other should have a linking verb. Underline the subject.

· Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment.
Practice Island: Sentence 83

· Fix the sentences:

-A family of rabbits scurry about.
-Would you like to go to the store with him and I?
-The soulful sounds of the music makes me happy.
-She and him are best friends.

· Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment.
Practice Island: Sentence 100

· Sentence Island: What are the four types of sentences? Write one of each, using the correct end punctuation.
